## Thread: ACUCOBOL and NETWORK — 5 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### ACUCOBOL and NETWORK

- **From:** "Gloria De Dionigi" <dedionigi@luigitosi.com>
- **Date:** 2002-06-20T16:19:07+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aesplv$b2a$1@newsreader.mailgate.org>`

```
Hi to everybody.

My code is written in ACUCOBOL.
I have a PC with NT workstation or 2000 (it's the same) with VISION files
on,
and a pc client (Windows 9X) with the programs.

When I try to open the files from the client I have to wait about 1 second a
file !!!
(Usually I use more then five files in a program)

I'm not using ACUSERVER, if I should  it would be all right.

If I put my files on windows 9X, or I have in my network all WINDOWS
NT/2000, or
all windows 9X there will be no problems.

Is it possible ?
What can I do except using ACUSERVER ? or limitate my PC Windows' version ?

I'll beg your pardon not only for the question but also for english: I'm
from Italy.

Gloria De Dionigi
```

#### ↳ Re: ACUCOBOL and NETWORK

- **From:** Doug Robinson <fdr@mytemyke.com>
- **Date:** 2002-06-20T11:35:43-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3d11f61c_1@news3.buffnet.net>`
- **References:** `<aesplv$b2a$1@newsreader.mailgate.org>`

```
On Thu, 20 Jun 2002 10:19:07 -0400, Gloria De Dionigi wrote:

> Hi to everybody.
> 
…[22 more quoted lines elided]…
> Gloria De Dionigi

Hmmmm Gloria;
   Something is going on here, lots of our customers run a peer to peer
network system and performance is rarely the big issue. We don't have anyone
using AcuServer, as that is only when you want to split the data across
several machines, (ie: Accounting on the IBM Netfinity, Manufacturing on
the Compaq Server etc.)  Having said all that I would encourage you to
move to a true client server system.  Put the Vision Files up on the
NT/2k box and install Terminal Server if you want to be a Micro$oft
customer. In the peer to peer environment someone will lock the record
for the rest of the users if they are sitting on it, (and go to lunch for
instance.) The real problem comes when the users are impatient and excute
the dreaded three finger salute (cntrl/alt/delete) while running the
application.  Now the Vision file system is likely to have a transaction
inflight and what do you think happens to the file integrity?
   My own first choice is to put the files on a Linux server (the old NT
box perhaps,)  install AcuThin and keep the Windows95 clients thin and
clean.  Another solution is to get a true networking system from Novell
and let that manage the Vision file access.  Third choice is to use
Windows NT/2k with Terminal server, Citrix, Tarantella etc.etc. 
   Your English is a lot better than my Italian :-}  
                                    Ciao
                                    Doug Robinson
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: ACUCOBOL and NETWORK

- **From:** "Gloria De Dionigi" <dedionigi@luigitosi.com>
- **Date:** 2002-06-21T10:00:24+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aeum92$cgn$1@newsreader.mailgate.org>`
- **References:** `<aesplv$b2a$1@newsreader.mailgate.org> <3d11f61c_1@news3.buffnet.net>`

```

Thank you so much Doug for your suggestions,
I find very interesting the fact that lots of your customers
run without significative problems a peer to peer network
system. This means that I can run too.
Now I'll try to talk directly with ACUCORP Italy.

Ciao e grazie, Gloria.
```

this before when reading files off of another windows machine in a
peer to peer network using Acucobol.  I have talked to Acucobol about

```

<a id="t-ee6aca462d"></a>
## Thread: MF Fileshare and CCITCP2 — 2 this month / 3 total

### MF Fileshare and CCITCP2

- **From:** graham@olympic.co.uk (graham)
- **Date:** 2002-06-20T18:29:24-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8a02b0a1.0206201729.70a01baf@posting.google.com>`

```
We've been testing the use of Fileshare on some of our customers sites
for a few weeks and have had no real problems so far, until now that
is!
It's on WINDOWS 95 (but then other W95 sites have worked). We've setup
via CCIINST first. We run CCITCP2 on the server, run FS on the server.
On the server the application works fine (with FHREDIR.CFG and
FS.CFG). On the workstation we've run CCIINST and then we run CCITCP2,
load our COBOL application and it fails with 9/125 (after several
seconds)

Swtich on CCITCP2 -d trace and it shows the machines do communicate
and without knowing the detail it looks sensible...there is are
BROADCAST_SEARCH AND BROADCAST_REPLYs, EXISTENCE and SERVICE request
from the workstation but there could be oddities - is there a
description of what should happen ? (at the moment I haven't got
access to a completely working environment) but also is there a way of
writing the trace to a file, at the moment I'm just saving and
printing from the screen ?

The only known difference in this setup is that the machine are not
set up with defined IP address they are allocated dynamically; however
the messages in the trace suggest that they are, at least, different
address if not really standard (10.0.0.7, e.g.). Frankly on the other
few sites we've tried it's worked (more or less) first time!!

Any suggestions gratefully receieved...

graham
```

#### ↳ Re: MF Fileshare and CCITCP2

- **From:** "Panos Zotos" <pzotos@syntax.gr>
- **Date:** 2002-06-21T17:16:04+03:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aevcft$5l8$1@usenet.otenet.gr>`
- **References:** `<8a02b0a1.0206201729.70a01baf@posting.google.com>`

```
Some thinks you can search:

- Why are you running ccitcp2 on the client machine? It should be sufficient
to run it on the server only
- Client needs to have a ccitcp2 environment variable defined, that contains
the IP or DNS of the server where ccitcp2 deamon runs. This is set by
CCIINST utility, but can you check autoexec.bat that it is there indeed?
Otherwise you can simply define it in autoexec as : set ccitcp2=IP or DNS of
server
- If you are using DHCP, then make sure that the ccitcp2 env.var (both in
the server and the client) only contains DNS names and not IPs

- If you need to search deeper, you might want to look at the winsock
version you are using. Some older versions do not work correctly. I'm not
sure if you can find winsock updates for Windows 95 any more. After all,
Windows 95 itself is not an enviroment that is now supported by Microsoft,
so it would worth moving to 98 or newer.

Ho these will help



"graham" <graham@olympic.co.uk> wrote in message
news:8a02b0a1.0206201729.70a01baf@posting.google.com...
> We've been testing the use of Fileshare on some of our customers sites
> for a few weeks and have had no real problems so far, until now that
…[25 more quoted lines elided]…
> graham
```

## Thread: COBOL FAQ — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Meta: FAQs, group policy, charter`](topics.md#meta)_

### COBOL FAQ

- **From:** wmklein@ix.netcom.com (William M. Klein)
- **Date:** 2002-06-21T09:22:31+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol,news.answers,comp.answers
- **Message-ID:** `<computer-lang/cobol-faq_1024651249@rtfm.mit.edu>`

```
Archive-name: computer-lang/cobol-faq
Posting-Frequency: twice a month
Last-Modified: 2002/05/06
Version: 3.03
URL: http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm
Reply-To: wmklein@ix.netcom.com


                            COBOL FAQ
                  (Frequently Asked Questions)

Last-Modified: Spring 2002

The full COBOL FAQ can be viewed at:

  http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm

If you have comments, corrects, deletions, or additions, please send
private email to:

    wmklein <at> ix.netcom.com

Toward the top of the FAQ is a pointer to the appendix listing the most
recent significant updates and changes.

This FAQ answers such questions as (and addresses topics such as):

 - Where can I get a COBOL compiler? 
 - Is there a free COBOL compiler? 
 - Where can I contact... ?
 - What is happening with the draft of the next COBOL Standard and what
is in it? 
 - What about OO COBOL? 
 - Books about COBOL
 - COBOL Tools
 - What can/should I post in the COBOL newsgroups?
 - What about USAGE? COMP? Storage for data in xyz format? etc?
 - How do I get started with COBOL? Where can I get education?
Tutorials? Etc

and much, MUCH more
```

<a id="t-3bca77ae24"></a>
## Thread: How to create portable print files from MicroFocus NetExpress? — 5 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### How to create portable print files from MicroFocus NetExpress?

- **From:** philhickling@compuserve.com (Phil Hickling)
- **Date:** 2002-06-21T08:02:21-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<6a371be2.0206210702.190c150d@posting.google.com>`

```
Any ideas please on how to produce print files that are both printable
and viewable on the screen, from a MicroFocus NetExpress application? 
I want to be able to include different fonts, underlining, bold, page
throws, etc., and possibly pictures.  I need to be able to print the
files myself, and email them to clients for them to view or print as
they choose, using standard tools they would already have.  Some ideas
include:
1)  Create plain asci text file, including printer control characters.
2)  Create a Word document by calling MS Word using OLE Automation.
3)  Create an html file, using html codes to change font etc.
4)  Use a Report Writer such as Crystal Reports.
5)  Create a .pdf file.

Any other ideas?  What would be best?  What do other COBOL users use?

But each of these methods have problems, at least for me!:
1)  Include printer control characters:  produces good prints, but is
not viewable on the screen, without extra software packages (such as
Visual PCL/2 from www.visual.co.uk).  (It is probably possible to
write a viewer using COBOL, but I am not sure how?)
2)  Calling Word:  very laborious, every action such as changing fonts
takes several commands.
3)  html:  I have experimented, and it has potential, but also seems
laborious.
4)  Report Writer:  I have no knowledge of them.
5)  .pdf:  again have no knowledge of how to do this.

Many thanks in advance.
Phil Hickling.
```

#### ↳ Re: How to create portable print files from MicroFocus NetExpress?

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2002-06-21T16:42:11+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<DHIQ8.452$cE5.29@nwrddc02.gnilink.net>`
- **References:** `<6a371be2.0206210702.190c150d@posting.google.com>`

```

You could create 'standard' rich text files. On screen, display in a
richedit control and the formatting is automatic . To print, send to MS word
*or any other software* which handles 'standard' rich text - and the
formatting is automatic.

MS-word format is a lot like rich text, but does have some differences, so
why not go with the more 'universal' Rich Text?
```

#### ↳ Re: How to create portable print files from MicroFocus NetExpress?

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-06-21T17:14:46+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D135F8D.97525C3@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<6a371be2.0206210702.190c150d@posting.google.com>`

```


Phil Hickling wrote:

> Any ideas please on how to produce print files that are both printable
> and viewable on the screen, from a MicroFocus NetExpress application?
…[26 more quoted lines elided]…
> Phil Hickling.

Taking your options :-

1) I don't really think you want to start writing a 'viewer' do you <G>

2) Calling Word very laborious - but not anymore laborious than using
PC_PRINT to do the same thing. Now here's the trick - all the *resuable*
stuff that you want to keep invoking for different reports -  put it into
"Print Classes" where you can send invokes from your calling program with
a set of parameters - now if you aren't comfortable with OO this is
*definitely* a non-starter but it would be the way to do it.

3) HTML - can't help - but again to get over the "laborious" put
repetitive stuff into
"Print Classes"

4) Don't know the product, but Crystal Report seems like a good option.

5) PDF - no knowledge.

Option (2) above, Word as an OLE,  is the route I intend to go, but
haven't even started yet. To give you an idea what you can do, I have
turned the PC_PRINT routines into an invokeable class. If interested in
taking a look to see how you could use a similar concept invoking Word
from COBOL (tied in with that example from Wayne Rippin), let me know and
I'll send you a copy.

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

#### ↳ Re: How to create portable print files from MicroFocus NetExpress?

- **From:** "Mike Kinasz" <mkinasz@cbspayroll.com>
- **Date:** 2002-06-22T05:35:35+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<H0UQ8.33730$EP.2666@sccrnsc03>`
- **References:** `<6a371be2.0206210702.190c150d@posting.google.com>`

```
The website you mention has another product which will convert your (since
you say you get good prints I assume you might be using it) PCL into
PDF.....(i use it and love it and i don't work for them)   then you just
spawn that converted file off into Acrobat Reader using a Windows API like
ShellExecute.

Good Luck,
Mike Kinasz

news:6a371be2.0206210702.190c150d@posting.google.com...

```

#### ↳ Re: How to create portable print files from MicroFocus NetExpress?

- **From:** "Gianni Spano" <softline2000@tin.it>
- **Date:** 2002-06-22T14:34:54+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20285920550e811bb2993e3186c260e0.40368@mygate.mailgate.org>`
- **References:** `<6a371be2.0206210702.190c150d@posting.google.com>`

```
Hello

I suggest you to visit www.pdf995.com.
You can also download the demo (a nag screen appears) and try
the product.
It is a small pdf device driver that allows you to generate
a Pdf file from any application, simply choosing through the
printer dialog.
Also there is an option to set the name of Pdf file in a way
you have no modify it. Or you can set the Pdf file name
directly each time you print a document.
It costs about $20. No more expensive and very easy to use.

I don't know Net Express, i'm using Fujitsu Powercobol and i
use this method to obtain the preview of my print works.
The pdf995 allows you to start Acrobat reader after the file
is created. This setting must be done with the pdfedit program
downloadable from the site above. 

Gianni

### NetCobol - API Call

- **From:** "R. Hendriks" <R.Hendriks_1@kub.nl>
- **Date:** 2002-06-22T14:56:10+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<af1s2m$k2u$1@mailnews.kub.nl>`

```
Hi there,

I've downloaded the API-Guide (allapi.net) and am trying to figure out how
to make api calls from fujitsu's netcobol. It just can't get it to work. The
project builder always gives the error that it has an unresolved external.
I've inserted the user32.lib file in the project so it should find the
function (here the SetClipBoardDataA function). I think there's a problem
with the arguments I pass with the api call. I haven't been able to find a
good description of API calls in Fujitsu's documentation (just to examples
and that's not enough).

Does anyone have a good reference for me? Thanx.

Rik Hendriks
```

#### ↳ Re: NetCobol - API Call

- **From:** "Gianni Spano" <softline2000@tin.it>
- **Date:** 2002-06-22T14:41:35+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<417183aae5340ebbaf0a1fc0de8e4307.40368@mygate.mailgate.org>`
- **References:** `<af1s2m$k2u$1@mailnews.kub.nl>`

```
Hi Rik,

You probably need to put the string "ALPHAL(WORD)" on the script 
properties of project (down the form name, tipically Mainform).
I use continuously Api call using Powercobol and it is easy to do.
If you need something, let me know.
Do you need to call the "SetClipBoardDataA" function?
Send me you project and i will take a look.
I'm preparing a sort of "Api Superbible for Powercobol".
When it will finished i will post a message on the forum.

Gianni

Sorry about the copy-paste api call. I was under the impression that I had
to hard code this functionality. I've checked whether CTRL-C and CTRL-V
worked just like that (put two textboxes on a form and ran it). Don't know
what I did wrong then, but it works fine. No need for the SetClipBoardData
know. Perhaps in the future when moving objects.

Conclusion: basic copy-paste functionality is indeed build-it!

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2002-06-23T11:31:59+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<PkiR8.3461$bt.1365@nwrddc04.gnilink.net>`
- **References:** `<af1s2m$k2u$1@mailnews.kub.nl> <417183aae5340ebbaf0a1fc0de8e4307.40368@mygate.mailgate.org> <af2cqe$pfp$1@mailnews.kub.nl> <7_3R8.348917$Gs.27008080@bin5.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com> <af46ii$ean$1@mailnews.kub.nl>`

```
R. Hendriks <R.Hendriks_1@kub.nl> wrote in message
news:af46ii$ean$1@mailnews.kub.nl...
> Sorry about the copy-paste api call. I was under the impression that I had
> to hard code this functionality. I've checked whether CTRL-C and CTRL-V
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> Conclusion: basic copy-paste functionality is indeed build-[in]!

Yes, it is; however, some applications may subclass the edit controls (text
boxes) and disable it. For applications you create, you must go out of your
way to do so.

That is, if you want to use ctrl-c, ctrl-v to provide data transfer between
your applications, it will work 'no muss, no fuss, no bother'; but you need
to test if you want to use one of your applications and another application.

(That said, I've only seen a handful of applications where it does not
work).

(Also, this tells you that if you want to disable "built in copy-paste" it
is doable.)

MCM
```

## Thread: CRT-Cursor — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### CRT-Cursor

- **From:** "Joseph Ostreicher" <josephos@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 2002-06-23T12:34:27-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3d15fa2a@news.mhogaming.com>`

```
Im in the middle of reading "Thane's Teach your self COBOL in 24 hours
book", I must say it sounds like simple stuff, the book is really easy to
understand, however, there is one page which I think is not written clear
enough.

He is talking about CRT and CURSOR entries under special-Names, I honestly
don't get it, if someone can please explain the following lines in plain
English, I would really appreciate it, give me as much details as possible.

 special-names.
     crt status is keyboard-status
     Cursor is cursor-position.

 working-storage section.
 01  keyboard-status.
     03 Accept-Status pic x.
     03 function-key pic x.
     03 system-use pic x.
 01  cursor-position.
     03 cursor-row    pic 9(2) value 1.
     03 cursor-column pic 9(2) value 1.

Also, is his saying that when using cursor settings, the cursor appears at
the field, with starting position closest to, but not less then???

Joseph
```

#### ↳ Re: CRT-Cursor

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-06-24T20:55:13+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D177921.6F35F5BE@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<3d15fa2a@news.mhogaming.com>`

```
Joseph Ostreicher wrote:
> 
>  special-names.
>      crt status is keyboard-status

The CRT status is basically what caused the ACCEPT ... AT statement to
return to the program.  The ACCEPT waits for the user to key into a
field (or fields).  When the user wants the program to do something he
ends the input with the enter key, or esc key or a function key or mouse
click or ...

It may be necessary to set up what can be used to terminate the ACCEPT
using a configuration utility or by the program calling routines.

>      Cursor is cursor-position.

This has two functions, the first is to set the cursor to a particular
place within the fields to be accepted, the second is to know where the
cursor was when the accept terminated.

The cursor is where typing will put characters.

For example I may want to accept 3 fields with:

    01  Field-Group.
        03  FILLER     PIC X(20) VALUE "  Customer Number: [".
        03  In-Cust    PIC X(8).
        03  FILLER     PIC X(52) VALUE "]".
        03  FILLER     PIC X(20) VALUE "    Customer Name: [".
        03  In-Name    PIC X(30).
        03  FILLER     PIC X(30) VALUE "]".
        03  FILLER     PIC X(20) VALUE "          Address: [".
        03  In-Address PIC X(30).
        03  FILLER     PIC X(30) VALUE "]".
    01  Field-Block REDEFINES Field-Group   PIC X(240).

        DISPLAY Field-Block   AT 0101
   *    this will display all 240 characters onto 3 lines at top of
screen     

        MOVE 0221      TO Cursor-Position
        ACCEPT Field-Group    AT 0101
   *    this will only accept the 3 non-FILLER fields with the
   *    cursor starting in the field In-Name
   *    the user can move to any of the 3 fields but not to
   *    anywhere else on the screen

        IF ( cursor-Position > 0300 )
   *        the cursor was in the 3rd field when the used completed
input
            ....

        IF ( Function-Key = 3 )
   *        the user pressed the F3 key to end the input 


>  working-storage section.
>  01  keyboard-status.
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> the field, with starting position closest to, but not less then???

The cursor can only be within one of the fields - either a field defined
in SCREEN SECTION or a non-FILLER within a group accept (as above) or a
simple field accept.  If the position is set to be outside the fields
(eg MOVE 1645 TO Cursor-Position) then it will be positioned within the
closest field.
```

### Performance problem due to MF Cobol?

- **From:** avannieuwenborg@hotmail.com (Anita van Nieuwenborg)
- **Date:** 2002-06-24T06:32:32-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<407f48c.0206240532.19045d18@posting.google.com>`

```
We have migrated a Cobol/CIsam application to MF-Cobol and Oracle.
The original application was developed using MPB-Cobol (Visual cobol?,
a compiler that has been bought by MF and then discarded) in the new
environment we use MF Server Express.

The performance is not exactly as we expected. If I check my batch
process I see that most CPU-Time is used by the cobol process (70
minutes) and only 5 minutes by the seperate Oracle process. The
original program took only 4 minutes :-(

Can anyone shed some light on this? Anyone tried something like this
before?

Regards

Anita
```

## Thread: Performance problem due to MF Cobol? — 2 this month / 3 total

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Performance problem due to MF Cobol?

- **From:** avannieuwenborg@hotmail.com (Anita van Nieuwenborg)
- **Date:** 2002-06-24T06:47:39-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<407f48c.0206240547.7b8cbe61@posting.google.com>`

```
We have migrated a Cobol/CIsam application to MF-Cobol and Oracle.
The original application was developed using MPB-Cobol (Visual cobol?,
a compiler that has been bought by MF and then discarded) in the new
environment we use MF Server Express.

The performance is not exactly as we expected. If I check my batch
process I see that most CPU-Time is used by the cobol process (70
minutes) and only 5 minutes by the seperate Oracle process. The
original program took only 4 minutes :-(

Can anyone shed some light on this? Anyone tried something like this
before?

Regards

Anita
```

#### ↳ Re: Performance problem due to MF Cobol?

- **From:** philhickling@compuserve.com (Phil Hickling)
- **Date:** 2002-06-28T03:56:33-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<6a371be2.0206280256.561a820@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<407f48c.0206240547.7b8cbe61@posting.google.com>`

```
All I can say is that I use MicroFocus COBOL NetExpress (not
ServerExpress) for a system with up to 150 users, running on an NT
Network, and we get good performance.  Some speed trials I ran, whilst
probably 60-80 users were connected, could read through MF COBOL
indexed files, with some rewrites, at over 300,000 transactions per
minute.  Other tests, again over a network but with a single user,
reached 766,000 sequential reads per minute.  I was impressed - and
surprised!  Normal performance with variety of different programs
running, accessing different bits of data is probably not anywhere
near as fast as this, but is adequate.

Any idea how many, and what sort of reads or other transactions you
are doing in the 70 minutes?  I would be interested.

Phil Hickling
```

distribution function.
It is probably the original source of the code in MatLab.
statlib is at:
http://lib.stat.cmu.edu
AS 241 is in Fortran; I expect it to be better than the code in Excel.
I think I also have it at my ozemail web site (see signature below).

Cheers

#### ↳ Re: NORMINV in simple Computer Languages

- **From:** epc8@juno.com (E P Chandler)
- **Date:** 2002-06-24T18:28:55-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7f64e2ff.0206241728.43faa39b@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<hBJR8.1384$Q12.41109@news4.ulv.nextra.no>`

```
"Hogne" <hogne@neteland.net> wrote in message news:<hBJR8.1384$Q12.41109@news4.ulv.nextra.no>...
> I'm in need of an NORMINV function for using in a mainframe system.
> The Computer language is COBOL, but other language may also be of interest.
…[21 more quoted lines elided]…
> Hogne Neteland

A standard numerical approximation is given in chapter 26 of
Abromowitz & Stegun, "Handbook of Mathematical Functions", equations
26.2.22 and 26.2.23.

A Google serach on "Inverse Normal CDF" pulled up several examples,
the first one returned is in JavaScript, courtesy of

Jan Deleeuw (deleeuw@galton.math.ucla.edu)

p = form.prob.value
t = (p > .5) ? (1 - p) : p
s = Math.sqrt(-2.0 * Math.log(t))
a = 2.515517 + (0.802853 * s) + (0.010328 * s * s)
b = 1 + (1.432788 * s) + (0.189269 * s * s) + (0.001308 * s * s * s)
u = s - (a / b)
form.zval.value = (p < .5) ? (- u) : u

Of course you need to scale the returned Z value. Good luck with the
log and square root functions. The ? & : is a "C" construction for
if/then/else. Also note that the range of s is quite large, so fixed
point may not be a good representation.
```

#### ↳ Re: NORMINV in simple Computer Languages

- **From:** "David Jones" <dajxxx@ceh.ac.uk>

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

- **References:** `<hBJR8.1384$Q12.41109@news4.ulv.nextra.no>`

> I'm in need of an NORMINV function for using in a mainframe system.

interest.
>

…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> It's probably not possible to make an accurate calculation, but if
I'll get
> an calculation that is as good as the one in Microsoft Excel, I'll
be
> satisfied.


If you are prepared to translate from Fortran, then Algorithm AS111
( in Applied Statistics (1977) Vol 26 No 1 ) might be a place to
start. You can also find the source code on the internet in many
places I expect, for example at statlib --http://lib.stat.cmu.edu/

David Jones
```

#### ↳ Re: NORMINV in simple Computer Languages

- **From:** jsuebersax@yahoo.com (John Uebersax)
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T08:56:45-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.basic.misc,comp.lang.cobol,sci.stat.math
- **Message-ID:** `<f6f3f967.0206260756.3ee5dd18@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<hBJR8.1384$Q12.41109@news4.ulv.nextra.no>`

```
"Hogne" <hogne@neteland.net> wrote in message news:<hBJR8.1384$Q12.41109@news4.ulv.nextra.no>...

> I'm in need of an NORMINV function...The Computer language is COBOL, 
> but other language may also be of interest.
...
> It's probably not possible to make an accurate calculation, but if I'll get
> an calculation that is as good as the one in Microsoft Excel, I'll be
> satisfied.

You can find a simple Basic program that uses one of Abramowitz &
Stegun's formulas at:

http://ourworld.compuserve.com/homepages/jsuebersax/probit.txt

A more accurate algorithm (Fortran, but translatable to Basic) can be
found at:

http://ourworld.compuserve.com/homepages/jsuebersax/papers.htm

> Im also interested in calculations based on table views.

I guess you mean that in a sense like SQL Tables--but there is a
similar term "table lookup" that refers to a method for handling
normal and inverse normal cumulative functions.  If that's what you
mean, my experience is that the polynomial approximations (e.g.,
Abramotitz & Stegun's formulas) are better than table loookup.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
John Uebersax, PhD             (858) 597-5571 
La Jolla, California           (858) 625-0155 (fax)
email:  jsuebersax@yahoo.com

Statistics:  http://ourworld.compuserve.com/homepages/jsuebersax/agree.htm
Psychology:  http://members.aol.com/spiritualpsych
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```

 @OPTIONS MAIN

 SCREEN SECTION.
 01 MAIN BLANK SCREEN foreground-color 6 background-color 3.
     03 line 1 column 34 value "String Test" underline, reverse-video.
     03 LINE 3 COLUMN 1 VALUE "Enter Expression".
     03 line 3 column 19 pic x(10) using expression-in.
     03 line 5 column 1 value "First-Term".

     03 line 8 column 1 value "Unstring-pointer".
     03 line 8 column 20 pic 9(2) using unstring-pointer.
     03 line 9 column 1 value "Total".
     03 line 9 column 13 pic z(5) from Total.
 procedure division.
 unstringg-start.
     display main
     accept main
     unstring expression-in delimited by "+" or "."  or "*"  or "/" into
first-term delimiter in
     operation count in unstring-pointer
     end-unstring
     add 2 to unstring-pointer
     unstring expression-in delimited by "=" into second-term pointer
unstring-pointer
     end-unstring
     add first-term second-term giving total.
     display main

Try using reference modification to clear the PIC 9 item.

03 WS-ITEM PIC 9(08).

MOVE SPACES TO WS-ITEM (1:LENGTH OF WS-ITEM).

The compiler will treat it as if it were PIC X(08).

HTH....

Bill

#### ↳ Re: NetCOBOL for Linux

- **From:** thaneh@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T21:10:01-07:00

```
I'd like to help with a Clarification.  Some time ago, Fujitsu renamed
their Suite of tools to "NetCOBOL".

Don't confuse COBOL for .NET, the Native Windows compiler and the
various Unix conpilers offered.  All of these make up the NetCOBOL
family.  However - the Linux compiler is a native Linux compiler. 
Based on the speed and performance of the Fujitsu Unix compilers, I'd
wager that this thing screams.

## Thread: Differnece between Cobol and Cobol II — 23 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Differnece between Cobol and Cobol II

- **From:** "Dave's Work and Job" <davidhacker@cox.net>
- **Date:** 2002-06-25T12:18:17+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<dcZR8.79718$hF5.3494061@news2.east.cox.net>`

```
I'm getting back into IBM COBOL after a few years of doing other stuff.
What's the main differences between the Old COBOL and COBOL II (the Y2k
Fixed version).
Thanks
```

#### ↳ Re: Differnece between Cobol and Cobol II

- **From:** scomstock@aol.com (S Comstock)
- **Date:** 2002-06-25T12:31:23+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20020625083123.18277.00000052@mb-bg.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<dcZR8.79718$hF5.3494061@news2.east.cox.net>`

```
Dave's Work and Job writes...

>I'm getting back into IBM COBOL after a few years of doing other stuff.
>What's the main differences between the Old COBOL and COBOL II (the Y2k
>Fixed version).
>Thanks
>

One thing in common: they're both obsolete and unsupported.

Check the archives on this group over the last 12-18 months.

After COBOL came COBOL/370, then COBOL for MVS & VM, then COBOL for OS/390 &
VM, now IBM Enterprise COBOL for z/OS and OS/390. Then of course there's the
Visual Age COBOL versions for various platforms. And that's just the IBM
compilers.

Kind regards,


Steve Comstock
Telephone: 303-393-8716
www.trainersfriend.com
email: steve@trainersfriend.com
256-B S. Monaco Parkway
Denver, CO 80224
USA
```

#### ↳ Re: Differnece between Cobol and Cobol II

- **From:** "David Robinson" <david@nighttime.demon.invalid>

"Dave's Work and Job" <davidhacker@cox.net> wrote in message
news:dcZR8.79718$hF5.3494061@news2.east.cox.net...
> I'm getting back into IBM COBOL after a few years of doing other stuff.
> What's the main differences between the Old COBOL and COBOL II (the Y2k
> Fixed version).

For me, personally, END-IF and not having to finish every sentence with a
'.'.

The number of days I've had ruined due to a  &$%*ing misplaced '.' when
trying to construct nested IFs in COBOL I.

Isn't COBOL I = ANSI COBOL'74 and COBOL II = ANSI COBOL'85? I don't think
COBOL II was the Y2K Fixed version, rather that a load of legacy apps needed
updating to account for Y2K.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Differnece between Cobol and Cobol II

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** Edward Reid <edwardreid@spamcop.net>
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T08:09:51-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<01HW.B93F274F010A034B10E07880@news-east.usenetserver.com>`
- **References:** `<4a5S8.73650$Ny6.4152507@bin2.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com> <20020625200214.17668.00000157@mb-mm.aol.com>`

```
On Tue, 25 Jun 2002 20:02:14 -0400, Ross Klatte wrote
> By the way, what's the meaning of DISPLAY WITH LOW-VALUES? 
> (Or, maybe it's DISPLAY WITH LOWER-BOUNDS??) 

WITH LOWER-BOUNDS is a Unisys (Burroughs) extension that deals with the 
method of passing parameters between COBOL and other languages. It's 
essentially a compiler directive -- it has no semantic effect but its 
presence or absence allows the generated code to be compatible with 
interlanguage CALLs.

DISPLAY means what it always does in a data item definition. In many 
Unisys shops, snippets of code have been passed around containing 
something like

  01 MY-DATA  PIC X(80) DISPLAY WITH LOWER-BOUNDS.

Because the WITH LOWER-BOUNDS is seldom understood, the entire line is 
taken as a magic incantation (which is basically true). Also, 
programmers in these same shops never write USAGE DISPLAY (that being 
the default). These factors -- the sharing of a magic incantation and 
the gratuitous presence of a seldom-used element within the incantation 
-- combine to lead to the assumption that DISPLAY WITH LOWER-BOUNDS is 
a phrase, when in fact it is two unrelated phrases.

There, that probably muddies that obscure point a bit more.

Edward Reid
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Differnece between Cobol and Cobol II

- **From:** "Howard Brazee" <howard.brazee@cusys.edu>
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T13:20:53+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<afcf3b$fv7$1@peabody.colorado.edu>`
- **References:** `<dcZR8.79718$hF5.3494061@news2.east.cox.net> <064afa.jqc.ln@nighttime.demon.invalid> <4a5S8.73650$Ny6.4152507@bin2.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com>`

```

On 25-Jun-2002, "JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com> wrote:

> There are purists who insist that there should be only one period per
> paragraph and that on a line by itself. The French have a word for people
> like this - but I forget what it is.

And there are purists who insist that every sentence should have a full stop
period, and it should never be in a line by itself.  Those are the standards
where I work.   Occasionally I would like a period in a line by itself, but
other than that, I am in this camp.
```

## Thread: [kernel32] could not find the calling program — 3 msgs

### [kernel32] could not find the calling program

- **From:** "R. Hendriks" <R.Hendriks_1@kub.nl>
- **Date:** 2002-06-25T16:15:45+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<af9trp$jjc$1@mailnews.kub.nl>`

```
I'm (still) trying to make a working api call. The compiler and linker make
the files required without any error, but when running the .dll file that is
made the kernel32.dll report [could not find the calling program]. Where
should I look for an answer/solution to the message?

Rik Hendriks
```

#### ↳ Re: [kernel32] could not find the calling program

- **From:** "JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2002-06-25T21:19:20+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<s75S8.437940$Oa1.30879489@bin8.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com>`
- **References:** `<af9trp$jjc$1@mailnews.kub.nl>`

```

"R. Hendriks" <R.Hendriks_1@kub.nl> wrote in message
news:af9trp$jjc$1@mailnews.kub.nl...
> I'm (still) trying to make a working api call. The compiler and linker
make
> the files required without any error, but when running the .dll file that
is
> made the kernel32.dll report [could not find the calling program]. Where
> should I look for an answer/solution to the message?
>
> Rik Hendriks

Look here by posting the code.

The most common error is that API calls are case sensitive. If you're not
careful, COBOL will fold lower case to upper before calling.
```

<a id="t-70aea10774"></a>
## Thread: Recursive COBOL call is illegal — 9 msgs

### Recursive COBOL call is illegal

- **From:** samhunt90@hotmail.com (Sam)
- **Date:** 2002-06-25T07:24:02-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<e20518e0.0206250624.788655d@posting.google.com>`

```
Hi,

I'm getting the error message "Recursive COBOL call is illegal"
because it is in fact a recursive call (i.e., pgm a calls pgm b which
calls pgm a), but that's the way it's supposed to be.

I'm using Microfocus Object Cobol, Version 4.0.38.  The only solution
I can think of is two differently named copies of the same program (it
will only be called a maximum of two times). However, that is
obviously a last resort solution.

Is there any other way around this?

Thanks,
SamHunt90
```

#### ↳ Re: Recursive COBOL call is illegal

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-06-25T12:07:23-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<afa80n$1dg$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<e20518e0.0206250624.788655d@posting.google.com>`

```
Although, support was changed in later versions (those currently supported)
of Micro Focus COBOL, I think that version ONLY supports recursive
programs - for programs with a Local-Storage Section defined (it does NOT
need any data items in it).  Obviously, if you WANT "instance-specific"
data, then you must use Local-Storage (not Working-Storage) section.
```

#### ↳ Re: Recursive COBOL call is illegal

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-06-25T18:30:26+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D18B75D.5A9D075@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<e20518e0.0206250624.788655d@posting.google.com>`

```


Sam wrote:

> Hi,
>
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
>

I don't know M/F Object COBOL, (I use Net Express), but I assume you are
CALLING and not INVOKING. As it is an M/F product you may want to check
on line help for CHAINING  and also RECURSION.

Not part of the future COBOL 200X, but the CHAIN syntax seems appropriate
to what you want to do.

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

#### ↳ Re: Recursive COBOL call is illegal

- **From:** lsunley@mb.sympatico.ca
- **Date:** 2002-06-25T23:56:14+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ZpzG4UNLyRNq-pn2-4db5Hw5dWb6j@thishost>`
- **References:** `<e20518e0.0206250624.788655d@posting.google.com>`

```
On Tue, 25 Jun 2002 14:24:02 UTC, samhunt90@hotmail.com (Sam) wrote:

> Hi,
> 
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

If you put a "local storage" section in the program the recursive call
will be allowed. Read up on the use to local storage to store data 
that is specific to the particular call instance.
```

#### ↳ Re: Recursive COBOL call is illegal

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T07:02:17+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D1958E9.537BBA47@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<e20518e0.0206250624.788655d@posting.google.com>`

```
Sam wrote:
> 
> I'm getting the error message "Recursive COBOL call is illegal"
> because it is in fact a recursive call (i.e., pgm a calls pgm b which
> calls pgm a), but that's the way it's supposed to be.

Recursive calls are illegal, you should not have a design that does
this.
 
> I'm using Microfocus Object Cobol, Version 4.0.38.  The only solution
> I can think of is two differently named copies of the same program (it
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> Is there any other way around this?

You could put an ENTRY point in a and call that from b.

You could split a into x and y where x is a small control module that
calls y, then x call b which calls y.

You could do a 'reverse call' where a calls b repeatedly with b setting
a flag that is passed in the call.  If the flag is 'X' then a stops
calling b, if the flag is 'Y' then a performs some code that is what you
need to do when 'b calls a'.  So instead of 'b calling a' it sets the
flag to 'Y' and does a EXIT PROGRAM. and then carries on when the next
call is made to it.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Recursive COBOL call is illegal

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-06-25T17:58:03-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<afasia$sk3$1@slb3.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<e20518e0.0206250624.788655d@posting.google.com> <3D1958E9.537BBA47@Azonic.co.nz>`

```
By "illegal", Richard "means" non-conforming to the ANSI/ISO '85 Standard.

A) They are EXPLICITLY legal in the draft of the next COBOL Standard

B) All Micro Focus products for MANY years (I don't know since when) have
documented them as "legal" (but as an extension). (This is relevant - as the
initial question was about a Micro Focus product)

C) Many (not all) OTHER current COBOL compilers also specifically allow
recursive programs.

  ***

As indicated earlier, the "solution" to the original problem is (probably)
to simply put a "dummy" Local-Storage (also currently an extension) header
in the program - to be called recursively.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Recursive COBOL call is illegal

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-06-27T08:07:54+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D1AB9CA.8432B34D@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<e20518e0.0206250624.788655d@posting.google.com> <3D1958E9.537BBA47@Azonic.co.nz>`

```
> By "illegal", Richard "means" non-conforming to the ANSI/ISO '85 Standard.

Actually it was MF that called it 'illegal'.  Even when it is 'legal' in
their compiler it is still "non-conforming to the ANSI/ISO '85
Standard". So, no, that is NOT what I meant.

> A) They are EXPLICITLY legal in the draft of the next COBOL Standard

And I would still say "you should not have a design that does this"
(where 'this' is a calls b, b calls a).

In fact many of my systems have potentially recursive calls.  For
example a stock enquiry can list the product codes for selection or the
same list of codes (eg when entering orders) can activate the enquiry,
but the implementation specifically eliminates actual recursion. ie
Order-Entry -> code list -> stock-enquiry -> code list (doesn't
activate).
 
> B) All Micro Focus products for MANY years (I don't know since when) have
> documented them as "legal" (but as an extension). (This is relevant - as the
> initial question was about a Micro Focus product)

I had it on early release with version 2.4 in 1990.  It is a requirement
for Windows API programs due to call-backs.
 
> As indicated earlier, the "solution" to the original problem is (probably)
> to simply put a "dummy" Local-Storage (also currently an extension) header
> in the program - to be called recursively.

That may well 'solve' the error message, but it won't make the programs
work.  In fact I find this entirely bad advice.  Making recursive calls
involves far more than 'simply putting in a LOCAL-STORAGE' especially
when this is a just a 'dummy' one.

Recursion has to be carefully designed into the program so that data
doesn't get corrupted.  It also must be made quite clear that this is
happening so that future maintainers understand the issues.

Given that there are several ways that should solve the problem without
recursion then I re-emphasize he "should not have a design that does
this".
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Recursive COBOL call is illegal

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T15:52:22-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<afd9p6$lq2$1@slb5.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<e20518e0.0206250624.788655d@posting.google.com> <3D1958E9.537BBA47@Azonic.co.nz> <3D1AB9CA.8432B34D@Azonic.co.nz>`

```
As usual, I don't understand your point Richard.

The original note stated that they HAD designed the application to use
recursive programming (intentionally).  Therefore, the only thing they
should need to do is add the Local-Storage header.  OBVIOUSLY, if there
original design was "flawed" and they were incorrectly planning on using
recursion, that would mean they had/have a design flaw.  I was taking for
granted, that the programmer had designed is program correctly and only need
to know how to "access" this documented (not illegal - but an extension)
feature of the compiler in question.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Recursive COBOL call is illegal

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-06-27T13:00:31+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D1AFE5F.3D29EC83@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<e20518e0.0206250624.788655d@posting.google.com> <3D1958E9.537BBA47@Azonic.co.nz> <3D1AB9CA.8432B34D@Azonic.co.nz> <afd9p6$lq2$1@slb5.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
William M. Klein wrote:
> 
> As usual, I don't understand your point Richard.

No, as usual, you don't seem to.    ;-)

> The original note stated that they HAD designed the application to use
> recursive programming (intentionally).  

No. It didn't state they they had "designed it to use recursion". It
only stated that program 'b' had wanted to call 'a', I suspect that it
came as a surprise that this was recursive. There is certainly no
indication that they _intentionally_ wanted it to use recursive
programming - where did you see this ? I interpreted this that they
naively fell into recursion and wanted to avoid it.

It should also be noted that they hadn't got this to actually work yet. 
It could be assumed that this had not been tried before (otherwise they
would know how to do it).  So it is not a 'design' based on experience,
but may be a kludge.

The fact that his "last resort" was to have two copies of 'a' indicates
that recursion is not part of the design at all, and he didn't ask for
'another way around' the error message, he actually asked for "another
way around" recursion other than needing to use two copies. Several of
which I had provided.

> Therefore, the only thing they
> should need to do is add the Local-Storage header. 

If they actually appreciated the issues that can occur with recursion
then they would already know that LOCAL-STORAGE may be required
independently of it being considered necessary by the compiler.

That is: the lack of LOCAL-STORAGE is indicative that 'program a' was
not _designed_ for recursion, and it is only 'supposed to be' from the
point of view of 'program b'.

> OBVIOUSLY, if there
> original design was "flawed" and they were incorrectly planning on using
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> feature of the compiler in question.

You shouldn't take anything for granted.

A more careful reading should show you that he wanted to avoid
recursion.
```

## Thread: COPY REPLACING compile in DB2 help — 4 msgs

_Topics: [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax) · [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### COPY REPLACING compile in DB2 help

- **From:** e1-npfq-sb09-n8jt@emailias.com (Perry)
- **Date:** 2002-06-25T10:04:04-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<1a0174a8.0206250904.6e20321f@posting.google.com>`

```
Does anyone know of a way to get the DB2 pre-compiler to correctly
interpret the COPY REPLACING command so that fields in this copybook
could be used as host variables in a DB2 SQL statement?

COPY ALPHA REPLACING ==:TAG:== BY ==WS-==.
=============================================
01  ALPHA-GROUP.
    05  :TAG:TIMESTAMP-FIELD       PIC X(26).
    05  :TAG:TEXT-FIELD            PIC X(40).
=============================================

EXEC SQL
    SELECT TIMESTAMP_FIELD, TEXT_FIELD
      INTO :WS-TIMESTAMP-FIELD, :WS-TEXT-FIELD
      FROM TABLE
END-EXEC

The ALPHA copybook doesn't get pulled in with the DB2 pre-compile, but
this would be fine if the copybook fields were not used as host
variables.

Any ideas?

Thanks!!!
```

#### ↳ Re: COPY REPLACING compile in DB2 help

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-06-25T12:13:20-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<afa8br$aag$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<1a0174a8.0206250904.6e20321f@posting.google.com>`

```
My *guess* is that this does NOT work - UNLESS - you are using the most
recent IBM COBOL compiler, i.e. IBM Enterprise COBOL for z/OS & OS/390.

In this version (or with IBM COBOL for OS/390 & VM V2R2 - with the
appropriate PTFs) you can use an "integrated" DB2 precompiler that should
handle this correctly.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: COPY REPLACING compile in DB2 help

- **From:** "Larry Kahm" <lkahm@bellatlantic.net>
- **Date:** 2002-06-25T23:37:02+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<y87S8.25479$cE5.14771@nwrddc02.gnilink.net>`
- **References:** `<1a0174a8.0206250904.6e20321f@posting.google.com> <afa8br$aag$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Are the fields in the copybook being used for anything other than host
variables?

'Cause I wonder what other value would you use to replace TAG (besides the WS-)
that you would even need this construct?

Larry Kahm
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: COPY REPLACING compile in DB2 help

- **From:** "Brian Walker" <walkerbrian@optusnet.com.au>
- **Date:** 2002-06-30T22:27:41+10:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3d1ef8db$0$31826$afc38c87@news.optusnet.com.au>`

<a id="t-50073eb886"></a>
## Thread: any pointers to using COBOL for OS/390 TEST(,,SEPARATE) — 8 msgs

_Topics: [`Mainframe, z/OS, JCL, CICS`](topics.md#mainframe)_

### any pointers to using COBOL for OS/390 TEST(,,SEPARATE)

- **From:** bo774@FreeNet.Carleton.CA (Kelly Bert Manning)
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T01:10:20+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<afb49s$7r4$1@freenet9.carleton.ca>`

```
First off, let me say that I don't expect to ever see the Debug Tool. 

The decision to decouple it from the compilers as a separate cost product
under LE means that it will never be bought for my site. COBTEST and
PLITEST were great, I even combined them with IMS BTS to do interactive
source code level debugging of IMS MPPs, alternating between simulated 
IMS screens, BTS command line mode screens, and COBTEST/PLITEST screens
with adjustable areas for displaying source, variables being monitored,
and the PLITEST/COBTEST command log. Who needed Exediter, etc. I don't 
expect to ever see that again.

I'm only interested in the new LE abend handling functions which are
independent of the Debug Tool.

The SEPARATE option seems like something to look at for IMS Message
Processing Region, to reduce load module bloat in address spaces where
many different OLTP programs are trying to stay around for reuse. CICS 
regions are also notorious for going Short On Storage.

To me the simple solution would have been to specify a DDname to be used
for a library containing members written to SYSDEBUG while compiling.
So far I haven't been able to find any mention of one.

The manuals say that the name of the "separate debug data set" is
recorded in the load module, which seems fine for first level
debugging, but is useless once the source, object code, DB2 Database
Request Modules, etc. get migrated to a Unit Test, Integration test,
or Production environment. In the worst case a production abend could
end up trying to use a new debug file for a new version still under
development.

The debugging manual suggests that the debug tool would prompt for 
debug dataset name if it can't find it, and also has some suggestions
about overriding defaults when doing batch testing.

Neither of these suggestions seems to be of any use for using a Separate
Debug Library Data Set member to get enhanced information in routine
production batch where the library names are all different from the 
ones used for development.

We use seperate High Level Indexes for each migration level, to help
to avoid having unauthorized staff promote source and object code.
Our philosopy is to migrate what was tested at lower levels, not to
recompile it and hope that it behaves the same as what was tested.
```

#### ↳ Re: any pointers to using COBOL for OS/390 TEST(,,SEPARATE)

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T08:00:50-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<afcduf$uuq$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<afb49s$7r4$1@freenet9.carleton.ca>`

```
1) COBTEST was a "cost-added" feature for VS COBOL II since (at least) the
late 80's.  So there is nothing new in this.

2)  I don't understand WHAT use you think you will ever get out of SYM
information (in the same load module or in a separate dataset) *if* you
don't use the Debug Tool?  Can you elaborate?

  ***

If you look at:
 http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/cgi-bin/bookmgr/BOOKS/igypg205/2.4.52?



it says,

"There are two ways that the TEST option can improve your formatted dumps
from Language Environment:

Use the NOSYM suboption of TEST to have line numbers in the dump that
indicate the failing statement, rather than just an offset.

Use the SYM suboption of TEST to have the values of the program variables
listed in the dump.

With NOTEST, the dump will not have program variables and will not have a
line number for the failing statement. "

Is this what you are after?  If so, I assume that you know that using any
value but NOTEST *forces* on NOOPT - and I would NEVER recommend that for
production code.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: any pointers to using COBOL for OS/390 TEST(,,SEPARATE)

- **From:** Colin Campbell <cmcampb@attglobal.net>
- **Date:** 2002-06-27T13:45:32-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D1B796B.2C6723A9@attglobal.net>`
- **References:** `<afb49s$7r4$1@freenet9.carleton.ca> <afcduf$uuq$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Bill,
SYM data is also useful if a program abends.  It causes the failing statement
number to print in both the message file and in CEEDUMP.  In addition, CEEDUMP
will produce a formatted dump of the Data Division.  (Without that, CEEDUMP
seems almost totally useless to me!)

"William M. Klein" wrote:

> 1) COBTEST was a "cost-added" feature for VS COBOL II since (at least) the
> late 80's.  So there is nothing new in this.
…[78 more quoted lines elided]…
> >
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: any pointers to using COBOL for OS/390 TEST(,,SEPARATE)

- **From:** Colin Campbell <cmcampb@attglobal.net>
- **Date:** 2002-06-27T14:18:49-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D1B8138.5ED720E6@attglobal.net>`
- **References:** `<afb49s$7r4$1@freenet9.carleton.ca> <afcduf$uuq$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Bill,
I tried to reply once already; I hope this doesn't show up twice.

SYM information is useful even without Debug Tool, because having it at the
time of an abend results in the source line number where an abend occurred
appearing in the message file (SYSOUT) and in CEEDUMP.  (This is in addition to
the offset into the program.)

Then, CEEDUMP generation will include a formatted listing of the abending
program's Data Division.  Without this output, I consider CEEDUMP basically
useless to most programmers.  With it, I consider that a SYSUDUMP is not
needed, and shops that have Abend-Aid, DumpMaster, or other such tools, are
close to wasting their money.  Programmers can usually solve their problems
with these two items.
Colin

"William M. Klein" wrote:

> 1) COBTEST was a "cost-added" feature for VS COBOL II since (at least) the
> late 80's.  So there is nothing new in this.
…[78 more quoted lines elided]…
> >
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: any pointers to using COBOL for OS/390 TEST(,,SEPARATE)

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-06-27T17:14:31-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<afg2oa$3jb$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<afb49s$7r4$1@freenet9.carleton.ca> <afcduf$uuq$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net> <3D1B8138.5ED720E6@attglobal.net>`

```
Thank you  Colin.

I also wanted to acknowledge a correction to my original note sent to me by
private email.

If you specify *either* NOTEST or TEST(NONE ...) - then you may still use
the OPT compiler option.
```

#### ↳ Re: any pointers to using COBOL for OS/390 TEST(,,SEPARATE)

- **From:** "Larry Kahm" <lkahm@bellatlantic.net>
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T15:18:29+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9XkS8.11104$bt.4406@nwrddc04.gnilink.net>`
- **References:** `<afb49s$7r4$1@freenet9.carleton.ca>`

```
Actually, SEPARATE is a sub-option of the TEST compile-time option.  Does your
current compile process use the TEST option for production code?

You can specify exactly what library you want the file to be place in with the
SYSDEBUG DD statement.

For example:    //SYSDEBUG  DD  DISP=SHR,DSN=DAVIN6.PDPAK.SIDEFILE(ANYPROG)

And, as you noted, when you use the TEST compile-time option, the compiler
stores the name of the debug file in the object module.  There are several
methods for pointing to the appropriate library when you need to.  One
recommendation is to make certain that your SCM (software change management)
system is updated to move the sidefile to production along with the load module.
However, the ability to access the contents of the sidefile is only appliable to
and used by the Debug Tool.

If you want enhanced abend detection, then you should really be looking at IBM's
Fault Analyzer.

For more information about both of these products, please take a look at a
recent IBM Redbook:  Introduction to the IBM Problem Determination Tools
(SG24-6296).

A copy can be found at
http://publib-b.boulder.ibm.com/Redbooks.nsf/RedbookAbstracts/sg246296.html?Open

Larry Kahm
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: any pointers to using COBOL for OS/390 TEST(,,SEPARATE)

- **From:** bo774@FreeNet.Carleton.CA (Kelly Bert Manning)
- **Date:** 2002-06-27T01:13:54+00:00

- **Message-ID:** `<afdosi$q6i$1@freenet9.carleton.ca>`

```

Found that one a while back. Too bad someone didn't think of using a similar
DDname at run time to specify a concatenation separate debug file libraries.
>

…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> and used by the Debug Tool.

So SEPARATE is really not of any practical use in production IMS MPPs 
and CICS programs, which are the ones where SEPARATE would probably have 
the most value.
> 
> If you want enhanced abend detection, then you should really be looking at IBM's
> Fault Analyzer.

I'm not so ambitious, I just want to not have to keep showing COBOL developers
and problem analysts how to use the pseudo assembler listing and the reported
offsets to find the failing insruction.

For PL/I the GOSTMT option does the same, and getting them to use that
reduced the traffic stream to my (DB Admin) desk.

I was hoping that the formated variable portion would help to avoid the
BLL nightmare. I've used at least 8 IBM cobol compilers from mainframe
DOS and VS2 back in 1977 to LE COBOL.

> 
> For more information about both of these products, please take a look at a
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…

#### ↳ Re: any pointers to using COBOL for OS/390 TEST(,,SEPARATE)

- **From:** Colin Campbell <cmcampb@attglobal.net>
- **Date:** 2002-06-27T14:11:18-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D1B7F76.3D90FD3D@attglobal.net>`
- **References:** `<afb49s$7r4$1@freenet9.carleton.ca>`

```


Kelly Bert Manning wrote:

> First off, let me say that I don't expect to ever see the Debug Tool.
>
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
> expect to ever see that again.

If your shop has COBTEST and PLITEST, it paid higher license fees for each
compiler to get them.  With Debug Tool, you would just order one of the
compilers as a "full function" version, and the Debug Tool would be
included.  The nice thing is that it supports both COBOL and PL/1 (plus
C/C++, if anyone cares).  So, it's three for the price of one, and learning
it for COBOL, you'd know most of what you need to know to use it for another
language.  (I hope IBM folds in Assembler Language support someday, too.
They tell me that others have requested this as well.)

>
> I'm only interested in the new LE abend handling functions which are
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
> So far I haven't been able to find any mention of one.

In the COBOL for OS/390 & VM V2R2 Programming Guide, that is exactly what is
offered.  DDname SYSDEBUG may define a sequential or partitioned data set
(including PDSE), of LRECL 80 to 1024 bytes, RECFM=FB.

>
> The manuals say that the name of the "separate debug data set" is
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> development.

You could recompile for production, after all testing is complete, and
specify the "production" PDSE as the SYSDEBUG DSName.  If you migrate upward
using a Change Control tool like CA-Endevor, you could specify that the tool
should "generate" (meaning compile and program bind) the COBOL at the lowest
level, and again at the highest level, allowing you to keep "production"
SYMbol files inviolate until a new production load module is created.  The
recompile isn't dangerous in this case (my opinion, of course), because
you're under control of the same procedures at each level, and the compile
would use exactly the same options, etc.  It's a matter of being able to work
around the limitations of the feature.

>
> The debugging manual suggests that the debug tool would prompt for
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
>

If I were you, I'd campaign with my management for the "right tools to do the
job".  Be sure you outline the advantages of having the Debug Tool, and of
using the TEST option.  Oftentimes, managers can be brought around to
agreeing with their technicians.  Then, they'll push the request upward for
you to the guy who approves spending the dough
```

```
using the following, how would I code it, to get the input and output on the
same screen item

 working-storage section.
 01  Phone-number           pic 9(14)      value zero.
 01  Formatted-number       pic x(14)      value "(xxx) xxx-xxxx".
 01  Formatted-alt          pic x(8)       value "xxx-xxxx".
 01  The-edited-number      pic z(14)      value space.
 screen section.
 01 pe blank screen.
     03 line 1 column 1 value "Enter Phone Number".
     03 line 1 column 22 pic 9(14) using Phone-number.
/     03 line 1 column 1 value "Edited Phone Number".
/     03 line 1 column 22 pic x(14) from the-edited-number.
 procedure division.
 if-test-start.
     display pe
     accept pe


     if phone-number > 9999999
/ test above 9(7)
     inspect Formatted-number
     replacing First "xxx" by phone-number (1:3)
               First "xxx" by Phone-number (4:3)
               First "xxxx" by phone-number (7:4)
     move formatted-number to phone-number
     else
     inspect formatted-alt
     replacing First "xxx" by phone-number (4:3)
               First "xxxx" by phone-number (7:4)
     move formatted-alt to phone-number
     end-if
     display pe
```

### TOTEM + BARCODE

- **From:** mcapisani@sopragroup.com (Capisani malo)
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T00:14:20-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<c2abef82.0206252314.4a779c56@posting.google.com>`

```
French | Question | en francais

J'utilise TOTEM 6 (Patch 6.01) et je souhaiterai scanner des code
barre plutot que de saisir des caractï¿½res dans un Entry-Field.
Quelqu'un l'a t-il dï¿½jï¿½ eassayï¿½ ?

Merci

DE : MALO CAPISANI ( France )
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

English | Question | en anglais

I use TOTEM 6 (PATCH 6.01) and would like to scan BARCODE instead of
typing characters in an entry field. Is someone have tested it ?


THANKS

DE : MALO CAPISANI ( France )
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```

#### ↳ Re: TOTEM + BARCODE

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T19:30:06+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D1A082E.98E4E085@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<c2abef82.0206252314.4a779c56@posting.google.com>`

```
Capisani malo wrote:
> English | Question | en anglais
> 
> I use TOTEM 6 (PATCH 6.01) and would like to scan BARCODE instead of
> typing characters in an entry field. Is someone have tested it ?

I have systems that use barcode readers on PCs, serial terminals and RF
guns.  These typically use a 'keyboard wedge' connection so that
scanning a barcode is exactly like keying in the number - the computer
or application can't tell the difference.

Usually the barcode scanner can be set to add a 'tab' or 'enter' at the
end of a scan so that some applications can be done entirely from the
scanner without having to do keyboard entry.  As an aid to this a page
of common and useful barcodes can be attached close by the machine so
that the operator can scan this rather than have to put down the reader
just to hit a couple of keys.
```

> Hi-
>
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> Will Fujitsu 4.0 project mgr. AND power cobol work past 90 days if
installed
> properly?  It cripples itself when the user attempts to install and use
past
> 90 day trial ENTERPRISE version (right?).
> As long as I dont try to install enterprise version I should be ok (yes /
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> days using project manager and power cobol.  I have something I can use
this
> for.  I hope I
> actually found a bargain for once.  I want to deploy an exe file for a
small
> program.  No database
> engines or runtime versions.

Fujitsu does not create stand-alone EXE files. To put your application on
another machine, you'll need to install the Fujitsu run-time consisting of,
oh, many (hundreds?) of files and make many (dozens and dozens) of registry
entries. Building a 'distribution' for an application is pretty easy for a
production version of the compiler, but those crafty devils may have left
out the essentials for so doing on a student version.
```

Okay, I've been out of the COBOL arena the last few years, having been made into
a DBA.  Now I'm being loaned out to a project to do coding again (this is a good
thing -- I still enjoy coding).  I worked previously and extensively with the
old Micro Focus Workbench, but this is my first exposure to Net Express.

That's the background, here's the problem . .

Given the following snippet of code, taken from the .LIS file . . . .

  1360             05 TS-HH                        PIC 9(2)    VALUE 0.
  1361             05 FILLER                       PIC X       VALUE '.'.
  1362             05 TS-MI                        PIC 9(2)    VALUE 0.
  1363             05 FILLER                       PIC X       VALUE '.'.
  1364             05 TS-SS                        PIC 9(2)    VALUE 0.
  1365             05 FILLER                       PIC X       VALUE '.'.
  1366             05 TS-MILLI                     PIC 9(6)    VALUE 0.
  1367         01 TS-TIMESTAMP-R REDEFINES TS-TIMESTAMP        PIC X(26).
  1368  
  1369        *
  1370        ************************
  1371         01  lk-tns-strg                     pic x(64).
  1372         01  lk-userid                       pic x(64).
  1373         01  lk-pswd                         pic x(64).
  1374         01  lk-msg-txt                      pic x(64).
  1375         01  lk-rc                           pic s9999 comp.
  1376  
  1377         PROCEDURE DIVISION.
  1378             move 'xctt' to lk-tns-strg
  1379  
  1380             PERFORM 11000-Connect
  1381                    UNTIL WS-CONNECT-YES
  1382                       OR WS-TRY-COUNT > 24
  1383             END-PERFORM
  1384  
  1385  
  1386             EXEC SQL


I get this error, taken from the .LST file . . .
 
*          NOXREF                 NOZEROLENGTHFALSE      NOZEROSEQ
*          NOZWB
  1363     END-PERFORM
* 564-S***************                                                 (   0)**
**    A scope-delimiter did not have a matching verb and was discarded.
* MERANT Micro Focus Net Express     V3.1 revision 255 Compiler
* Copyright (C) 1984-2001 Micro Focus International Ltd. URN AXCGG/AA0/00000
*                                                        REF GNR-152060000AC
* Last message on page:    2
* Total Messages:     1
* Unrecoverable :     0                    Severe  :     1
* Errors        :     0                    Warnings:     0
* Informational :     0                    Flags   :     0
* Data:       10434     Code:           0


So, what in the world is wrong with the END-PERFOR?  I've already checked that
it starts in col. 12.  Any why is the error msg saying it's in line 1363, which
is back up in working-storage a few lines before the beginning of the procedure
division.

<self-pity>
Egads, this makes me look like a rank beginner, and I've been in this business
over 20 years.  I built our company's first PC based development platform for
the mainframe, around MF products.  And now I can't even solve this???
</self-pity>

#### ↳ Re: Losing my mind over simple syntax (non)error

- **From:** epc8@juno.com (E P Chandler)
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T15:16:06-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7f64e2ff.0206261416.51cf87a3@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<3d1a01c5.22414660@ausnews.austin.ibm.com>`

```
>   1380             PERFORM 11000-Connect
>   1381                    UNTIL WS-CONNECT-YES
>   1382                       OR WS-TRY-COUNT > 24
>   1383             END-PERFORM

> I get this error, taken from the .LST file . . .
>  
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> **    A scope-delimiter did not have a matching verb and was discarded.

END-PERFORM delimits an in-line PERFORM. You are using a paragraph
name so you don't need it.
```

#### ↳ Re: Losing my mind over simple syntax (non)error

- **From:** epc8@juno.com (E P Chandler)
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T15:24:03-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7f64e2ff.0206261424.4fe1a2ed@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<3d1a01c5.22414660@ausnews.austin.ibm.com>`

```
>   1380             PERFORM 11000-Connect
>   1381                    UNTIL WS-CONNECT-YES
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>   1363     END-PERFORM

You're using a paragraph name so you don't need the END-PERFORM. This
delimiter would be appropriate for an in-line PERFORM:

MOVE 1 TO I.
PERFORM UNTIL I > 10
  DISPLAY I
  ADD 1 TO I
END-PERFORM.

is an in-line PERFORM.
```

#### ↳ Re: Losing my mind over simple syntax (non)error

- **From:** sff5ky@aol.comxxx123 (Sff5ky)
- **Date:** 2002-06-26T22:59:05+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20020626185905.13189.00000047@mb-mj.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<3d1a01c5.22414660@ausnews.austin.ibm.com>`

```
In article <3d1a01c5.22414660@ausnews.austin.ibm.com>,
spamdump@nospam.noway.nohow (Ed Stevens) writes:

>  1380             PERFORM 11000-Connect
>  1381                    UNTIL WS-CONNECT-YES
…[33 more quoted lines elided]…
>

I would guess that you are making one of the main mistakes that comes from
attempting to code to the new standards and only having pre-standards
experience.
(Standards referenced here is the '85 extensions of scope terminators.)
The END-PERFORM is a scope terminator for inline style performs.
Your code appears to be performing out-of-line to a sub paragraph.
```

#### ↳ Re: Losing my mind over simple syntax (non)error

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-06-27T07:35:16+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D1AB224.A228AE05@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<3d1a01c5.22414660@ausnews.austin.ibm.com>`

```
Ed Stevens wrote:
> 
>>   1380             PERFORM 11000-Connect
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> **    A scope-delimiter did not have a matching verb and was discarded.

The PERFORM syntax is either:

        PERFORM label ...

OR      PERFORM ...
           statement
        END-PERFORM

There is a specific rule that states END-PERFOM and label cannot exist
on the same PERFORM.
```

#### ↳ Re: Losing my mind over simple syntax (non)error

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-06-27T07:37:17+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D1AB29D.3A689A93@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<3d1a01c5.22414660@ausnews.austin.ibm.com>`

```
Ed Stevens wrote:
> 
>>   1380             PERFORM 11000-Connect
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> **    A scope-delimiter did not have a matching verb and was discarded.

The PERFORM syntax is either:

        PERFORM procedure-name ...

OR      PERFORM ...
           imperitive statement(s)
        END-PERFORM

There is a specific rule that states END-PERFORM and procedure-name
cannot co-exist on the same PERFORM.
```
